task.broken_reason: Task is well-formed: clear instruction, deterministic gold {"Landlocked":27.7,"Border":42.0}, F1+LLM-judge verifier. Caveat is real-world data-source flakiness, not a structural defect.

outcome.headline: Agent burned the full 600s budget classifying cantons then chasing an unavailable BFS xlsx dataset; never obtained any percentage data and never wrote answer.json before timing out.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: F1 over flattened JSON key-value pairs in /app/answer.json against gold {"Landlocked":27.7,"Border":42.0}, with a Claude-Haiku LLM-judge fallback when F1<1.0.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer.json was ever written; the agent was still probing BFS/PxWeb endpoints when it hit the 600s timeout, so the verifier found no answer file (reward 0.0).
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence on data acquisition
failure_modes.description: Repeatedly switched data-fetch strategies (wget, curl dam-api, pxweb api, opendata.swiss, wikipedia, asset html) against BFS endpoints that returned 500 / 'not yet available', never pivoting to synthesize canton percentages from the Google search snippets it already had, and never approaching a usable dataset before timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available

counterfactual.X: After the first failed downloads, pivot to extracting the 2023 cantonal migratory-background percentages from the Google search 'Cantonal Breakdown (2023)' results / Wikipedia, compute the two arithmetic means, and write /app/answer.json.
counterfactual.Y: A populated answer.json enabling a nonzero F1 or LLM-judge pass instead of a timeout with no deliverable.

failure_modes.name: Over-granular border classification wasting budget
failure_modes.description: Spent steps 4-13 fetching individual exclave/municipality Wikipedia pages (Kleinlützel, Rodersdorf, Metzerlen-Mariastein, Rafz) to re-verify canton international-border status that the step-3 search had already resolved to 15 cantons, consuming time that contributed to the timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Of the 26 Swiss cantons, **15** share a border with a neighboring country.

failure_modes.name: Border geography over-exploration

failure_modes.name: Non-convergence on data retrieval until timeout

counterfactual.X: Locate the correct FSO STAT-TAB px table for 'Ständige Wohnbevölkerung nach Migrationsstatus, Kanton' and query its JSON-stat data to extract each canton's 2023 percentage.
counterfactual.Y: Per-canton migration-background percentages, enabling the two group averages (Landlocked 27.7, Border 42.0) to be computed and written.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent data retrieval loop

The loop consumed the 600-second budget and produced no answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: A single step was not enough; recovery required abandoning repeated failed BFS access paths, obtaining or deriving all 26 canton percentages, computing the two simple group averages, and writing /app/answer.json before timeout.
counterfactual.Y: The run would have produced a valid JSON answer matching the verifier target instead of timing out with no answer file.

outcome.headline: The agent found the right topic and canton grouping but got stuck in a non-convergent data-retrieval loop and timed out without writing /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier checked that /app/answer.json exists, parsed it as JSON, compared flattened key-value pairs against {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0} with exact F1, and optionally used an LLM judge if F1 was not perfect.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No final answer file was produced; the run timed out while still exploring BFS/PxWeb data sources.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent official-data retrieval loop
failure_modes.description: After locating the relevant BFS table concept, the agent repeatedly tried unavailable asset URLs, scraped error pages, guessed PxWeb identifiers, and continued exploring until the 600-second timeout instead of converging on the numeric canton values or writing an answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: A single step could not fix the run because the agent still needed to retrieve or reconstruct the canton-level percentages, calculate both group averages, and write the JSON answer.
counterfactual.Y: The correct recovery required a strategy pivot away from repeated failing BFS asset/PxWeb guesses.

failure_modes.name: API response handling errors hid useful state
failure_modes.description: The agent mishandled the PxWeb API structure by using an absent key, then later suppressed the resulting exception and interpreted empty output as exploration progress, wasting remaining time.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Output: 'id'

counterfactual.X: At step 27, change the script to read db['dbid'] instead of db['id'] and print errors instead of suppressing them.
counterfactual.Y: The agent could have systematically inspected candidate PxWeb tables instead of losing time to empty/error outputs.

reward_hacking.evidence: The trajectory shows web searches and data-source probing, with no attempt to inspect benchmark answers or manipulate the verifier.

issues: The verifier script embeds the expected answer in plaintext as EXPECTED_ANSWER, which would be an answer leak if /tests were accessible during agent execution.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test_outputs.py contains EXPECTED_ANSWER = "{\"Landlocked\": 27.7, \"Border\": 42.0}" and performs exact key-value matching, so access to the verifier file would trivially reveal the solution.

outcome.headline: Timed out in repeated data-source exploration before writing /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier first checked that /app/answer.json existed, then parsed it as JSON and computed exact key-value F1 against {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0}, with an LLM judge fallback if exact F1 was imperfect.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No final answer file was produced. The agent classified cantons and identified candidate BFS table assets, but its downloaded /app/data.xlsx files were empty or 73-byte error messages and it timed out while still exploring the BFS/PxWeb API.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent data retrieval loop
failure_modes.description: After finding the relevant BFS table identity, the agent repeatedly tried brittle download URLs, asset pages, web searches, and PxWeb probes without converging on usable canton-level data or producing the required JSON before timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Analyzing PxWeb Structure: I've been examining PxWeb's architecture at BFS. My focus is the flat list of `dbid`s under `/api/v1/de/`, which lacks folder structures. I've noted that specific table metadata is accessed via `GET /api/v1/de/{dbid}`. Initial exploration indicates approximately 200 tables exist.

failure_modes.name: No deliverable artifact
failure_modes.description: The agent never wrote the required /app/answer.json. The local verifier would give zero immediately when that file is absent, regardless of partial research progress.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Error: /app/answer.json not found

counterfactual.X: Write a valid /app/answer.json before timeout, even if based on the best available computed or sourced values.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would have evaluated a concrete JSON answer instead of failing on a missing deliverable.

reward_hacking.evidence: No evidence of attempts to tamper with verifier state or exploit scoring; the trial failed through timeout and missing answer.json.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier is a straightforward exact JSON key-value check with LLM fallback; no structural exploit was used in the trial.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s harness timeout after spending almost all steps on border geography and failing BFS/PxWeb downloads, never writing /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.json; test_outputs.py parses it and scores F1 against gold {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0} (lines 8-12, 176-219), with LLM-judge fallback if F1 < 1.0
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.json; ~12 Wikipedia/shell steps classifying border cantons; ~19 shell steps downloading BFS assets (HTTP 500/404, empty files, "storage item master ... is not yet available") and probing PxWeb; final step was an in-progress PxWeb analysis message with no tool calls

failure_modes.name: No answer deliverable
failure_modes.description: Across 40 agent steps the trajectory never records a write to /app/answer.json (only mentioned in step-0 instructions). The run ended at step 40 with a planning message while still exploring PxWeb, and harbor raised AgentTimeoutError after 600s with verifier reward 0.0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write your final answer as a valid JSON dictionary to `/app/answer.json`.

counterfactual.X: Write {"Landlocked": <computed>, "Border": <computed>} to /app/answer.json once canton percentages are available (or from an alternate source).
counterfactual.Y: Continued API/download exploration through step 39 without ever creating /app/answer.json before timeout.

failure_modes.name: BFS download non-convergence
failure_modes.description: From step 14 onward the agent repeatedly tried BFS DAM/PxWeb fetches; observations returned HTTP 500/404, 0-byte files, or JSON {"message":"storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available"}, but the agent retried similar wget/curl paths (steps 16-18, 22, 35-36) instead of pivoting to a working source or computing from data already in web-search snippets.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: "message" : "storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available"

failure_modes.name: Border geography over-exploration
failure_modes.description: Steps 2-12 consumed most of the early budget on Wikipedia border minutiae (Bern vs France, Kleinlützel, Rodersdorf) even after step 2 web search already listed 15 border cantons; the agent only moved to migratory-background data at step 13.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Bern is landlocked.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: F1 matching on two rounded floats is brittle; the LLM-judge fallback (test_outputs.py lines 211-217) could accept semantically equivalent but numerically different JSON without full canton-level provenance, though this trial never submitted an answer.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s AgentTimeoutError while still exploring canton borders and BFS/PxWeb data sources, never writing /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: tests/test.sh:8-14 requires /app/answer.json to exist, then tests/test_outputs.py:176-219 parses it and scores exact key-value F1 against gold {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0} (LLM judge fallback if F1 < 1).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.json; only exploratory shell scripts, web searches, Wikipedia scrapes, and failed BFS asset/curl/PxWeb download attempts (0-byte or 73-byte error JSON responses).

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent data hunt
failure_modes.description: After step 2 already returned border vs landlocked canton lists, the agent spent the remaining budget on repeated Wikipedia border checks and BFS/PxWeb fetch attempts that returned HTTP 400/404/500, empty files, or {"storage item master for 32824411 is not yet available"}, without ever computing cantonal percentages or writing the required JSON.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: storage item master for 32824411 is not yet available

failure_modes.name: Border Wikipedia time sink
failure_modes.description: Step 2 web search already listed all 15 border cantons and 11 landlocked cantons, but steps 3–12 re-verified borders via per-municipality Wikipedia pages (Bern, Kleinlützel, Rafz, Rodersdorf) instead of moving on to 2023 migratory-background percentages.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: And Bern is landlocked.

counterfactual.X: Use the step-2 search canton groupings immediately and start downloading/parsing the BFS 2023 migration-status table (asset 32824411 / PxWeb).
counterfactual.Y: Re-scraped Wikipedia pages to re-confirm which cantons touch international borders.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares submitted JSON to embedded gold via deterministic F1; agent cannot see tests/ during the run. Failure here is missing deliverable, not a verifier loophole.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s harness timeout while looping on broken BFS/PxWeb data fetches and never wrote /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:9-14 requires /app/answer.json; test_outputs.py:176-218 parses it and scores F1 against gold {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0} (LLM judge fallback if F1<1).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Helper scripts and shell probes only; repeated failed BFS asset downloads (73-byte error JSON, HTTP 500 HTML); PxWeb API table enumeration; no final JSON answer file.

failure_modes.name: Official data source non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After a step-13 web search returned 2023 cantonal migratory-background summaries, the agent spent steps 14-40 retrying BFS Excel URLs, DAM API assets, and PxWeb catalog endpoints that returned HTTP 500, unavailable storage, or huge dbid lists without ever computing group averages or writing /app/answer.json.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available

counterfactual.X: After step 13, use available canton-level percentages to classify border vs landlocked cantons, compute equal-weighted arithmetic means, and write /app/answer.json.
counterfactual.Y: Kept hammering broken BFS static/DAM/PxWeb download paths until the 600s agent timeout.

failure_modes.name: Border geography rabbit hole
failure_modes.description: Before attempting migration statistics, the agent burned steps 2-12 on Wikipedia scraping to verify whether individual communes and exclaves (Bern, Kleinlützel, Rafz, Rodersdorf) constitute international borders, delaying data collection.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I've confirmed Rafz is in Zurich, which borders Germany.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier requires JSON key-value F1 match to fixed gold; LLM judge only upgrades near-matches, not a shortcut around computing correct canton averages.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s agent timeout while still probing BFS/PxWeb data sources and never created /app/answer.json, so the verifier scored 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh checks that /app/answer.json exists (lines 9-14); test_outputs.py parses it and scores exact key-value F1 against gold {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0}, with optional LLM-judge upgrade (lines 176-219).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.json; helper scripts (border.py, get_data.py, opendata.py, pxweb.py) and repeated failed BFS asset downloads returning JSON errors or empty files.

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: Across 40 agent steps the agent never emitted the required JSON deliverable at /app/answer.json. Execution ended with AgentTimeoutError after 600 seconds while the agent was still listing PxWeb dbids.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Retried broken BFS assets
failure_modes.description: After web search supplied the su-d-01.05.03.01.04 Excel link, the agent repeatedly curled BFS dam/master URLs that returned "storage item master ... is not yet available" or HTTP 500, instead of switching to the alternate 2021 asset URL surfaced in step 15 or computing from available summaries.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available

counterfactual.X: At step 18, download the working 2021 BFS asset (24367717) or another canton-level table, compute per-canton percentages, average by border/landlocked groups, and write /app/answer.json.
counterfactual.Y: Retried the broken 32547544/32824411 master endpoints and continued PxWeb API exploration without producing an answer file.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Pass requires a valid /app/answer.json with F1=1.0 on both canton-group averages; the LLM judge only applies after a parseable answer exists, so empty or missing files cannot be upgraded.

outcome.headline: The agent exhausted the 600s agent timeout exploring BFS/PxWeb and Wikipedia without ever writing /app/answer.json.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:9-13 requires /app/answer.json; test_outputs.py:176-209 computes exact-match F1 against gold {"Landlocked": 27.7, "Border": 42.0} with optional LLM-judge fallback
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.json; only shell probes, failed BFS asset downloads returning JSON errors, and PxWeb/Wikipedia scraping scripts.

failure_modes.name: BFS download retry loop
failure_modes.description: After identifying the su-d-01.05.03.01.04 spreadsheet, the agent repeatedly fetched BFS dam asset URLs that returned 73-byte JSON errors instead of pivoting to a working canton-level table and computing group averages.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: storage item master for 32547544 is not yet available

failure_modes.name: Wikipedia border-canton drift
failure_modes.description: Following a web search that already listed the 15 border cantons, the agent spent many steps scraping Wikipedia pages about exclaves and municipal borders rather than obtaining 2023 per-canton migratory-background percentages.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Does canton of Bern border France

counterfactual.X: Use the step-2 border-canton list and immediately query BFS for 2023 canton migratory-background percentages.
counterfactual.Y: Ran follow-up web and Wikipedia commands about whether Bern borders France and Kleinlützel exclaves.

issues: BFS bfsstatic dam asset URLs cited in search results returned "storage item master ... is not yet available" in this trial environment, which can block the intended official-data path.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test_outputs.py:211-217 can upgrade a sub-perfect F1 score to pass via LLM judge semantic equivalence.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
